query: How do I read the disk usage for my root mount and my CPU package temperature in an eww label? I tried {EWW_DISK[/].used_perc} and {EWW_TEMPS.coretemp Package id 0} and both throw parse/syntax errors.
expected_behavior: Routes to data access / bracket-vs-dot notation (DATA_ACCESS.md 'Common Mistake: Bracket vs Dot Notation', also SKILL.md Data Access)
Identifies wall 1: dot notation breaks on keys containing slashes or spaces — {EWW_TEMPS.coretemp Package id 0} fails because the spaces break parsing
Identifies wall 2: bracket notation needs the key QUOTED — {EWW_DISK[/]...} parses the bare / as division, not a string key
Gives the correct forms: {EWW_DISK["/"].used_perc} and {EWW_TEMPS["coretemp Package id 0"]}
Recommends wrapping with round() for clean display and notes the exact EWW_TEMPS key name is hardware-specific (check with eww state)
notes: Guards against two coupled bracket/dot-notation walls: (1) dot paths can't hold slashes or spaces, and (2) an unquoted key inside [] is parsed as an expression — a bare / becomes the division operator. Keys with special chars MUST use quoted bracket notation. A naive answer might quote one and not the other, or use dot notation for the spaced thermal-zone key.
